intent_questions.question: What behavior must be proven?
intent_questions.self_answer: GET /posts/1 from json-server should return HTTP 200 and the README example post body with id 1, title a title, and views 100.
intent_questions.risk_if_wrong: A weak model may test the wrong route or assert fields not present in the README contract.
intent_questions.how_to_override: Replace the route and expected output only with exact public repository evidence.

intent_questions.question: What is the system boundary: Runner target, Mocker dependency, hook host, or configuration-as-code?
intent_questions.self_answer: The Runner target is an externally-started json-server HTTP process on 127.0.0.1:3000; QaaS YAML does not start or clean that process.
intent_questions.risk_if_wrong: Process lifecycle could be incorrectly encoded as undocumented QaaS YAML.
intent_questions.how_to_override: Provide documented QaaS lifecycle support or keep startup in an external harness plan.

intent_questions.question: What public docs/schema path proves the capability exists?
intent_questions.self_answer: QaaS public docs/schema support HTTP Transactions, FromFileSystem data sources for payload and expected CSV bytes, HttpStatus assertions, and OutputContentByExpectedCsvResults assertions.
intent_questions.risk_if_wrong: The YAML could cite fields from stale examples instead of generated schemas.
intent_questions.how_to_override: Update docs evidence and rerun the selected candidate checker.

intent_questions.question: What inputs, outputs, and side effects prove success?
intent_questions.self_answer: Input is a GET request to /posts/1. Outputs are HTTP status 200 and JSON fields id=1, title=a title, views=100. Side effects should be read-only for this GET.
intent_questions.risk_if_wrong: A weak model may over-assert mutations, persistence, or unrelated endpoints.
intent_questions.how_to_override: Provide public side-effect evidence for any additional assertion.

intent_questions.question: Which negative, malformed, outage, retry, cleanup, and observability cases matter?
intent_questions.self_answer: Negative, malformed, retry, and observability cases remain blocked; this candidate covers only one README-backed happy path and cleanup planning.
intent_questions.risk_if_wrong: A weak model may claim broad API coverage from one happy-path route.
intent_questions.how_to_override: Add exact public negative-case and observability contracts before expanding assertions.

intent_questions.question: Which dependencies must exist: broker, HTTP endpoint, database, Redis, S3, Kubernetes, filesystem, credentials, ports?
intent_questions.self_answer: Dependencies are Node/npm access, an externally-started json-server process, db.json in the runtime working directory, and port 3000 availability; those dependencies are identified from public evidence but not live-validated.
intent_questions.risk_if_wrong: The candidate may be promoted where node/npm, port, or fixture placement is not available.
intent_questions.how_to_override: Provide validated dependency transcripts for the target environment.

intent_questions.question: What can run now, and what must be deferred?
intent_questions.self_answer: Static candidate validation can run now. QaaS template validation, live act/assert execution, cleanup proof, and live weak-model validation are deferred and block promotion.
intent_questions.risk_if_wrong: A weak model may confuse a candidate packet with an executable test.
intent_questions.how_to_override: Attach passing template, live, cleanup, airgapped, and strong-review evidence.

intent_assumptions.assumption: The README quick-start example is the public behavior contract for this candidate.
intent_assumptions.why_safe: It contains exact command, URL, route, and response body markers harvested from an immutable Git blob.
intent_assumptions.risk_if_wrong: The candidate may not match another json-server version or fixture.
intent_assumptions.how_to_override: Replace the selected contract with another immutable public blob.

intent_assumptions.assumption: Process lifecycle belongs to the external harness until QaaS docs prove otherwise.
intent_assumptions.why_safe: The public QaaS Runner docs describe HTTP transactions but not starting arbitrary Node processes.
intent_assumptions.risk_if_wrong: Startup or cleanup may be missed if a future QaaS lifecycle feature exists.
intent_assumptions.how_to_override: Provide public QaaS lifecycle docs and update the checker.

intent_assumptions.assumption: Generated schema evidence overrides stale quick-start assertion field names.
intent_assumptions.why_safe: The generated HttpStatus schema requires StatusCode and OutputNames.
intent_assumptions.risk_if_wrong: If runtime accepts aliases, the candidate remains conservative but still schema-aligned.
intent_assumptions.how_to_override: Provide updated generated schema or runtime validation transcript.

cases.scenario: Call README-backed json-server GET /posts/1 and assert the documented 200 response body.
setup: Start json-server externally with the candidate db.json using the README command.
Use get-post-one.bin as the byte payload driver for the GET transaction.
action: Run QaaS Runner against test.qaas.yaml after template validation is available.
assertions: OutputContentByExpectedCsvResults maps id, title, and views from posts-1.csv.
cleanup: Terminate the tracked json-server process tree.
Remove candidate session-data after live validation.
cases.blocked_reason: QaaS template, live act/assert, cleanup, and live weak-model validation have not passed.

cases.scenario: Keep Node process startup and cleanup outside QaaS YAML until public QaaS docs prove lifecycle support.
setup: Use candidate-runtime-plan.json as an external harness plan only.
action: Start and stop json-server through a tracked process runner, not through undocumented QaaS YAML fields.
assertions: Runtime plan remains blocked and never counts as executable promotion evidence by itself.
cleanup: Tracked process tree termination must be validated before promotion.
cases.blocked_reason: Process lifecycle and cleanup are planned but not validated.

cases.scenario: Tie every candidate field to immutable selected public contract evidence and QaaS public docs.
setup: Review selected-contract.json, README blob, and QaaS schema docs.
assertions: Candidate stays blocked until executable evidence exists.
No undocumented QaaS fields are introduced.
cleanup: No external resources are created by static candidate validation.
cases.blocked_reason: Static evidence does not prove live behavior.

assertions: Candidate Runner YAML uses schema-derived HttpStatus StatusCode/OutputNames.
Candidate body assertion uses documented FromFileSystem input bytes and OutputContentByExpectedCsvResults fields.
Candidate remains blocked until template, live QaaS, cleanup, airgapped, and strong-review evidence pass.

dependency_gates.blocked_reason: Live weak-model validation has not passed.

required_evidence: Public API, CLI, or runtime contract
C# build result when code artifacts exist
Live QaaS run/act/assert result when dependency gates are ready

cleanup: No live cleanup was performed during static candidate generation.
validation_sequence: Validate selected immutable public contract evidence.
Run QaaS template validation when a Runner host is available.
Run live QaaS act/assert against tracked json-server process.
Run live airgapped weak-model validation and strong review before promotion.

source_only_blockers.description: Live weak-model validation has not passed for this candidate packet.
required_evidence: Airgapped transcript with dry_run false and expected output contract preserved
source_only_blockers.unblock_instruction: Run D:\QaaS\_tools\weak-model-session.ps1 -Airgapped -All -ReasoningEffort none when quota/routing is available.

blocked_reason: Selected candidate passed lifecycle and QaaS template/live validation, but executable promotion remains blocked until live airgapped weak-model validation and strong review pass.

lifecycle_validation.command: npx --yes --package json-server@1.0.0-beta.15 json-server db.json ; GET http://127.0.0.1:3000/posts/1 ; terminate tracked process tree

validation_advisories.description: The quick-start example uses stale HttpStatus field names, while generated schema docs require StatusCode and OutputNames.
validation_advisories.resolved_by: schema-derived StatusCode/OutputNames template and live validation
